name: Abdullah ibn Wal
title: Worshiper of Tawwabin
shortDescription: A prominent Shia and leader of the Tawwabin uprising who stood on the front line in the battle against the army of Sham and was manfully martyred.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Wal al-Tamimi was among the well-known worshipers, ascetics, and heroes of Kufa. With his heart burning from the grief of not helping Imam Hussain (AS), he joined the core group of the Tawwabin uprising.

In the epic battle of 'Ayn al-Warda', where the small Tawwabin army faced the countless forces of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, Abdullah ibn Wal was a main commander. After the martyrdom of Sulayman ibn Surad and Musayyab ibn Najaba, the flag of command fell to him.

Taking the flag, Abdullah called his companions to persevere, saying: 'O people! Whoever desires the Hereafter, come to me.' With a drawn sword, he struck the heart of the army of disbelief.

Abdullah ibn Wal fought so bravely that the Syrian soldiers surrounded him from all sides, and finally, through strikes of spears and swords, he attained the great honor of martyrdom and his soul joined the martyrs of Karbala.
quote: (Upon taking the flag:) Whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter and meeting God, come to me.
